page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the arm64 ARMv9 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is provided via a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. The Nitro hypervisor manages the instance, which lacks hardware accelerators like GPUs.

Benchmark results show average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests scored 1706.99 and 6814.04 respectively, while Geekbench returned a single-core score of 1860.0 and a multi-core score of 5899.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 14.18 GB/sec. However, Passmark CPU encryption tests are poor, scoring 750.26, and Passmark CPU extended instructions are weak at 3679.01. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.36 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.66 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gd.xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for ARM-native workloads requiring local NVMe storage without the overhead of external network storage. The instance is suitable for static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 10.09 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring 6043.96 in Passmark database benchmarks. It is also capable of handling Redis operations, reaching 9.24 million operations per second. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 7B model are limited to 6.48 tokens per second.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server delivers average performance in stress-ng and Geekbench suites, with strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds of 14.18 GB/sec. However, it shows weak performance in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instructions. The instance is qualitatively cost-effective for ARM-native workloads requiring fast local storage, such as static web serving, Redis key-value databases, and general database operations, while being less optimal for LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering solid performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, integrated NVMe SSD local storage, and balanced performance for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory paired with a 237 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 14.18 GB/sec
Average performance for Redis operations and static web serving
Suited for ARM-native applications, database operations, and web servers
